model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, the current batch size only exposes the optimizer to a small fraction of the training set. Increasing batch size should cover much more data within the same step budget, and a modest learning-rate increase keeps optimization progress from slowing when each step uses a larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last change improved loss substantially, which suggests the fixed-step budget benefits from processing more examples per optimizer step. Pushing batch size closer to a full pass over the training set within 256 steps, while modestly scaling learning rate, is a direct follow-up to that signal.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last two successful steps both came from increasing data throughput under the fixed 256-step budget. Moving to batch size 256 should let the model cover the full training set and continue the same favorable trend without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Each successful step so far has come from increasing the number of examples processed within the fixed 256-step budget. A further batch-size increase is the most direct continuation of the strongest empirical signal in the run.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest repeated signal remains higher example throughput within the fixed 256-step budget. The last increase still improved validation loss, so a moderate additional batch-size step is the simplest next probe before changing architecture or optimization behavior.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch size has been pushed substantially upward, and the marginal gain from the latest increase was smaller. A modest learning-rate increase is a natural next step to better match the larger-batch regime without adding extra architectural or scheduling complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger learning-rate move regressed, so this step backs off to the better setting and adds a small amount of label smoothing. At the current high-data-throughput regime, mild smoothing is a simple regularization change that can improve validation loss without altering the model or training budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The warmup-plus-cosine schedule produced a large gain at the previous learning rate, which suggests the optimization is now much more stable. A moderate learning-rate increase is the most direct follow-up to test whether the improved schedule can support faster progress in the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The learning-rate increase remained beneficial after adding warmup, suggesting the current schedule can support a more aggressive step size. A further small increase is the cleanest next probe before introducing more structural changes.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning-rate increases have continued to improve validation loss under the warmup-cosine schedule, so the current regime has not obviously saturated yet. A further modest increase is the most efficient next test before changing model structure or regularization.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last learning-rate increase overshot slightly, so this step returns to the stronger setting and reduces weight decay. With only 256 optimization steps and an already aggressive schedule, lighter regularization may preserve more fitting capacity without changing the overall training dynamics.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization stack now looks strong, and recent scalar tuning gains have become smaller. A modest capacity increase is the next clean test: with large batches, warmup, and a higher learning rate already working well, the compact baseline may now be underfitting within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent capacity increase produced a large gain, so the model still appears architecture-limited rather than optimization-limited. Adding a third convolutional stage is a focused next test that increases representational depth while keeping the rest of the successful training recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The third convolutional stage produced a very large gain, which strongly suggests the model is still capacity-limited. A modest width increase is the cleanest follow-up: it expands representational power across all stages while preserving the now-successful optimizer, schedule, and depth settings.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider convolutional trunk regressed and also raised runtime substantially, so this step backs off that change and shifts extra capacity into the cheaper classifier head. This keeps the strong three-stage feature extractor while probing whether a larger MLP head can use those features better within the same training budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger classifier head did not help, so this step restores the previously stronger head size and tests slightly lighter regularization on the much better three-stage network. That is a simple follow-up around the current best recipe, with minimal disruption to the successful optimization setup.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}
